Metadata.ExomizerCommand: exomizer sfx 0x4800 "HARDBALL-4800" -Di_irq_exit=1 -s "inc $d030 lsr $d011" -f "w1: bit $d011 bpl w1 w2: bit $d011 bmi w2 jsr $fda3 rol $d011 dec $d030" -x "inc $d020" -o "HardBall!.prg"
Metadata.Notes: Removed the multiload protection which loaded the same code at $4700-$47EF and executed after each inning. The only free memory to store the code is at $0334-$03FF. As this is not enough memory to store the #$EF bytes, non-essential parts of the tape multiload routine are removed and used. 1. Tape multiload routine is located from $AF30 to $AFA1. 2. Kept essential loader code at $AF30 - JSR $C6F1 ($20, $F1, $C6), JSR $C05F ($20, $5F, $C0) and JSR $0FEF ($20, $EF, $0F). 3. Added the first #$66 bytes of the multiload code at $AF39 to $AF9E. 4. Added the next #$7F bytes at $037F with a JMP $037F at $AF9F. (The remaining bytes are a self-modifying wipe and RTS that can be ignored.) 5. Added an RTS at $03FF which is normally achieved by self-modifying code. 6. $A789 changed from BNE $A78E ($D0, $03) to BNE $A78B ($D0, $00) to bypass multiload code anti-tamper check. 7. $C689 changed from STA $7A ($85, $7A) to BIT $7A ($24, $7A) to bypass multiload failure check. 8. Moved data from $037F to $03FF to free memory at $0B7F with a restore routine added at $0B00. 9. Added JSR $0B00 ($20, $00, $B0) at $4800 to restore data before running game. (Restoring $037F - $03FF as part of an init routine would overwrite uncrunched start routine.)
